gallery_delete_confirm: Delete this detection and its files?
set_notify_new_daily: Notify first species of the day
set_notif_no_urls: No URLs configured — notifications will not be sent.
set_alerts_desc: Get notified when a critical threshold is exceeded.
set_notif_events_desc: Check the events you want to receive notifications for.
set_alert_svc_down: Alert when a critical service goes down
share_text_only_hint: Text only (no photo). To attach the image: 📋 Copy then paste, or 📲 Share on mobile.
set_save_conflict: Conflict: settings were modified by another tab. Reload and try again.
set_defaults_confirm: Reset all detection parameters to their default values?
set_defaults_applied: Default values applied — click Save to confirm
set_model_desc_mdata: BirdNET V2.4 + geographic filter — filters species by location and week
set_model_desc_mdata_v2: BirdNET V2.4 + geo filter V2 — improved location and week filtering
set_model_desc_v1: BirdNET V1 — older model, less accurate (legacy)
set_model_desc_perch: Google Perch V2 — 10,340 birds among 15K total species
set_model_desc_perch_fp16: Google — 10,340 birds, ~384 ms on Pi 5. Near-perfect quality vs original (top-1 100%, top-5 99%).
set_model_desc_perch_dynint8: Google — 10,340 birds, ~299 ms on Pi 5, ~700 ms on Pi 4. 4x lighter (top-1 93%).
set_model_desc_perch_original: Google — 10,340 birds, unmodified reference. Most accurate but heaviest (~435 ms on Pi 5).
dual_desc: Analyze each file with two models in parallel
dual_wait: Secondary model will load on next cycle (~5 min).
audio_sr_note: Output sample rate: 32,000 Hz (required by Perch V2, not configurable)
cal_instructions: Place both microphones side by side (< 5 cm), same direction. Capture lasts 10 seconds.
notif_rare_desc: Never seen or less than N total detections
notif_favorites_desc: Notify when a favorite species is detected (once per day per species)
notif_channel_desc: Configure the Apprise URL to receive notifications. Apprise supports 100+ services: ntfy, Telegram, Discord, Slack, email, etc.
notif_bird_alerts_desc: Get push notifications when a remarkable bird is detected. Notifications are sent via Apprise with a 5-minute cooldown between alerts for the same species.
notif_rare_long: Notification when a species with few total detections is heard. Great for uncommon species in your area.
notif_season_long: Notification when a species reappears after a long absence. Useful for detecting spring migrant returns.
notif_new_long: Notification when a species is detected for the very first time on this station. Rare and exciting!
notif_daily_long: Notification for each new species detected today. Can generate many notifications (20-30/day).
notif_favorites_long: Notification when one of your favorite birds is detected for the first time today. Set favorites on the Species page.
notif_sys_alerts_desc: Technical alerts when a system threshold is exceeded. Helps you react before detection quality is impacted.
notif_report_desc: Weekly summary sent every Sunday evening: species count, top 10, new species of the week.
review_none: No suspect detections for this period.
review_purge_warning: The following detections will be permanently deleted from the database and their audio files will be removed. This action cannot be undone.
species_desc: Control which species are detected. One scientific name per line.
species_include_desc: If filled, only these species will be detected.
fp_hint: Spectrogram generated from 3 seconds of mic input. Run again to refresh.
audio_denoise_desc: Attenuates constant background noise (wind, traffic, insects) using spectral gating. Requires scipy + noisereduce.
audio_denoise_warn: High values may attenuate faint bird calls.
audio_click_start: Click Start to display real-time audio levels.
audio_sum_desc: Mixes both channels into a single mono signal. Improves signal-to-noise ratio by +3dB since noise averages out while signal reinforces. Recommended when both mics capture the same scene.
audio_max_desc: Analyzes each channel separately and keeps the highest confidence score. Maximizes detection chances — if a bird is closer to one mic, its detection is retained. Ideal for covering a wider area.
audio_vote_desc: Requires the bird to be detected on both channels independently. Greatly reduces false positives since a noise spike on one mic is ignored. Recommended in noisy environments.
audio_ch_left_desc: Uses only the left channel (CH0). Simple choice if you have a single microphone plugged into the left input.
audio_ch_right_desc: Uses only the right channel (CH1). If your mic is plugged into the right input.
audio_strategy_desc: With two microphones, you can choose how to combine their signals for analysis. Each strategy has a different impact on detection quality and false positive rate.
audio_strategy_tip: Tip: if unsure, start with 'Maximum' to maximize detections, then switch to 'Vote' if you get too many false positives.
ag_desc: Adjusts software gain based on ambient noise. Observer mode: calculates without applying.
audio_pipeline_desc: Audio is processed in this order before BirdNET and Perch analysis. Green steps are active.
audio_highpass_desc: Cuts low frequencies (background noise, wind, traffic). Most birds sing above 1000 Hz. Increase the cutoff if you're near a road or in an urban area.
audio_lowpass_desc: Cuts high frequencies (insects, electronics). Most birds sing below 10 kHz. Lower to filter out high-pitched interference.
audio_noise_profile_desc: Record your environment's background noise (when no birds are singing). This profile will be subtracted from each recording before analysis — ideal for highway noise, HVAC, or any constant noise.
audio_denoise_auto_desc: Automatic noise floor estimation via statistical analysis. Less precise than a recorded profile, but works without configuration.
audio_denoise_disabled_by_profile: Disabled — the recorded noise profile is used instead (more accurate).
audio_rms_desc: Normalizes each recording's volume to a constant target level. Compensates for volume variations from wind, distance, or adaptive gain.
audio_overlap_desc: Controls the overlap of 3-second analysis windows. Higher overlap means more accurate detections but more CPU usage.
audio_profile_desc: Profiles contain preset settings for different environments. Duplicate a profile to create your own configuration.
fp_desc: Records 3 seconds from the microphone and displays the spectrogram before and after applying the filters above.
cal_instructions: Place both microphones side by side (< 5 cm apart), pointing in the same direction. Make sure no loud noise is present. Capture takes 10 seconds.
analyses_select_prompt: Select one or more species to explore their data.
narr_peak_at: Peak activity at {time}, representing {pct}% of daily detections.
narr_activity_range: Activity starts around {start} and ends around {end}, spanning {duration}.
narr_second_peak: A second notable peak appears around {time}.
narr_multi_hint: The rose chart and heatmap show data for {species}. The time series compares all species.
grp_select_prompt: Select an order or family to analyse the group.
grp_narr_period: Over the period {from} → {to}, the group {name} has {species} species with {total} detections.
grp_narr_dominant: The dominant species is {species} with {pct}% of detections.
grp_narr_peak: The group's peak activity is at {time}.
del_confirm_body: This will delete {count} detections and all audio files for "{name}". This action cannot be undone.
del_one_confirm:
Delete detection from {date} at {time} (confidence: {conf})?

The audio file will also be deleted.
spectro_idle_msg: Click Start to activate the spectrogram.
spectro_idle_desc: Audio comes from recent BirdNET MP3s — no conflict with ongoing analysis.
spectro_connecting_msg: Connecting to the Pi audio stream…
no_notable_obs: No notable observations in the last 7 days.
sys_confirm_stop_msg: Stop service "{name}"? This may interrupt analysis.
set_backup_stop_confirm: Stop the running backup? (rsync will resume on next run)
listen_spectro_hint: to listen and view the spectrogram
ebird_enable_text: To enable this section, get a free key at
ebird_then_configure: then configure it on the Pi:
ebird_no_notable: No notable observations in the last 7 days.
bw_id_in_url: The ID is visible in the URL:
wn_insuf_needsWeek: This card requires at least 7 days of detections. It will activate automatically once this period has passed.
wn_insuf_needsTwoWeeks: This card requires at least 15 days of detections to identify significant absences.
wn_insuf_needsMonth: This card requires at least 28 days of data to compute a reliable baseline.
wn_insuf_needsSeason: This card requires at least one year of data to compare seasonal peaks.
wn_insuf_needsGPS: GPS coordinates not configured. Set LATITUDE and LONGITUDE in /etc/birdnet/birdnet.conf.
wn_insuf_tooEarly: Not enough detections today yet. Check back in a few hours.
dash_baseline: Listening, analyzing and validating bird activity in real time.
update_how_title: On the Raspberry Pi: git pull && npm install && sudo systemctl restart birdash
pheno_pick_species_sub: Type a name above to view its observed phenology calendar.
pheno_migrant_sub: Long absence detected — species likely absent part of the year
pheno_disclaimer: These phases are inferred from your station detections, not from reference biological data. Specific breeding stages (display, laying, molt) are not detectable acoustically. Dates may vary by weather and latitude.
pheno_week_zoom_hint: Click a week to see details
sp_action_pheno_sub: Observed annual cycle · inferred phases · hourly activity
set_model_restart_warning: Model saved but engine may need manual restart
set_model_restart_error: Model saved but engine restart failed
spectro_filters_hint: Playback filters — modify real-time listening, not the original recording.
set_birdnet_license_notice: BirdNET is distributed under CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0 (non-commercial use). Downloading means you accept the license.
set_birdnet_accept: I accept the license and download
sys_copy_logs_desc: Copies the last hour of logs from all services to clipboard. Useful when reporting a bug.
avg_confidence_tooltip: Average confidence across all models. BirdNET (sigmoid) and Perch (softmax) scores use different scales — see per-model comparison below.
models_conf_caveat: Confidence scores are not directly comparable between models (different scales).
weather_corr_caveat: r = statistical correlation (association), not a cause-and-effect relationship. Values |r| < 0.2 hidden (not significant).
rare_tooltip: Species not recently observed in this region (source: eBird, last 30 days, 25 km). On a fresh install (< 30 days), no species is flagged as rare.
activity_tooltip: Total number of detections (3-second analysis windows scoring above the confidence threshold). Measures acoustic volume, not diversity.
top_species_tooltip: Ranked by total detection count (acoustic volume, not reliability). Highly vocal species or those detected in many analysis windows rank highest.
avg_confidence_multimodel: Not shown in multi-model mode (BirdNET and Perch use incomparable confidence scales). See the Models tab for per-model details.
bug_report_ph_desc: Steps to reproduce, expected vs actual behavior...
bug_report_auto_info: System info (version, browser, page) will be included automatically.
set_network_desc: Join the community network! Your station will appear on the map and share daily detection statistics (species, counts). No personal data is transmitted.
